cases.name: Basic sentiment classifier

queries.input: I love this product! It works great.
This is the worst experience I've ever had.
The food was cold and the waiter was rude.
The movie was okay, not great but not terrible either.
omg this is literally the best thing i've ever bought!!! 😍
meh. it does the job i suppose.
total waste of money, broke after two days :(

cases.spec: Classify the sentiment of the input text. Output exactly one word: positive, negative, or neutral.

cases.name: Spam detection

queries.input: WINNER!! Claim your FREE prize now, click here: bit.ly/xyz
Hey, are we still on for lunch tomorrow at noon?
URGENT! Your account will be suspended unless you verify your password immediately.
The quarterly report is attached, let me know if you have questions.
Congratulations, you have been selected for a $1000 gift card. Reply YES to claim.
hey its mike from accounting, can u resend that invoice pdf?
You have (1) pending reward waiting!! Act NOW >>> claim-prizes.biz

cases.spec: Classify the message as spam or ham. Output only: spam or ham.

cases.name: News topic classification

queries.input: Local team wins championship after dramatic overtime finish
Parliament passes new budget bill after lengthy debate
New smartphone chip promises twice the battery life
Five easy pasta recipes for weeknight dinners
Star striker signs record transfer deal
Chef's viral ramen hack has home cooks divided
Election results delayed amid recount in three states

cases.spec: Classify the headline into exactly one topic: sports, politics, technology, or food. Output only the topic word.

cases.name: Customer intent classification (few-shot spec)

queries.input: My order arrived damaged and nobody is answering my emails.
What sizes does this jacket come in?
I'd like to order the deluxe bundle please.
Is there a warranty on the blender?
yo quick q — does the black one come back in stock soon?
this is the 3rd time my delivery has been late. absolutely fed up.

cases.spec: Classify the customer message intent as one of: greeting, complaint, question, purchase. Examples: "Hi there!" -> greeting. "This thing broke after one day" -> complaint. "Do you ship to Canada?" -> question. "I'll take two of the blue ones" -> purchase. Output only the intent word.

cases.name: Language identification

queries.input: Guten Morgen, wie geht es dir?
Je voudrais un café, s'il vous plaît.
no entiendo nada de lo que dices, amigo

cases.spec: Identify the language of the input text. Output exactly one of: english, french, spanish, german.

cases.name: Emotion classification

queries.input: I can't stop smiling, we got the house!!
Are you KIDDING me?? They cancelled AGAIN?!
i just miss her so much, the house feels empty
I keep hearing noises downstairs and I'm home alone...

cases.spec: Classify the dominant emotion of the message as one of: joy, anger, sadness, fear. Output only the emotion word.

cases.name: Message urgency classification

queries.input: SERVER IS DOWN, CUSTOMERS CANNOT PAY!!
no rush, whenever you get a chance
Need the signed contract in the next hour or we lose the deal.
fyi the newsletter went out this morning

cases.spec: Classify the message urgency. Output exactly one of: urgent, not_urgent.

cases.name: Review star rating prediction

queries.input: Absolutely perfect, could not be happier!
Terrible. Broke immediately and support ignored me.
Pretty good overall, just a couple of minor gripes.

cases.spec: Predict the star rating from 1 to 5 implied by the review. Output only the number.

cases.name: NER: multiple person names

queries.input: Barack Obama met Angela Merkel in Berlin.
The duet features Taylor Swift and Ed Sheeran.
hey did u see what lebron james said about steph curry lol
Marie Curie won two Nobel Prizes; her daughter Irène Joliot-Curie won one.

cases.spec: Extract all person names from the text, comma-separated, as written in the text.

cases.name: NER: organizations

queries.input: Google and Microsoft are competing for the contract.
She left Goldman Sachs to join the World Health Organization.
NASA partnered with SpaceX on the launch.
my brother works at ikea and my sister is at deloitte

cases.spec: Extract all organization names from the text, comma-separated.

cases.name: NER: locations

queries.input: The flight from Tokyo to Paris stops in Dubai.
We hiked through Patagonia before flying home to Toronto.
The Amazon River flows through Brazil and Peru.
Snow closed the roads between Denver and Boulder.

cases.spec: Extract all location names from the text, comma-separated.

cases.name: NER: typed entities to JSON

queries.input: Tim Cook announced Apple's new campus in Austin.
Elon Musk flew to Berlin to visit the Tesla factory.
Serena Williams visited the United Nations in New York.
The mayor of Chicago met with representatives from Boeing.

cases.spec: Extract named entities from the text into a JSON object with exactly the keys "persons", "orgs", and "locations", each a list of strings as written in the text. Use an empty list when there are none. Output only the JSON.

cases.name: NER: entity type disambiguation

queries.input: I ate an APPLE with lunch.
APPLE reported record earnings this quarter.
She moved to WASHINGTON to work for the government.
AMAZON is hiring thousands of delivery drivers.
The AMAZON is the world's largest rainforest.

cases.spec: For the word written in ALL CAPS, output its entity type in this sentence. Output exactly one of: company, fruit, place, person.

cases.name: NER: drug names (medical)

queries.input: The patient was prescribed amoxicillin and ibuprofen.
Take paracetamol for the fever; avoid aspirin.
Metformin 500mg twice daily, plus atorvastatin at night.
doc put me on sertraline last month

cases.spec: Extract all drug names from the text, comma-separated.

cases.name: NER: medication and dosage to JSON
cases.spec: Extract the medication and its dose into a JSON object with exactly the keys "drug" (string, as written) and "dose_mg" (integer). Output only the JSON.

cases.name: NER: legal case parties

queries.input: In Smith v. Jones, the court ruled for the defendant.
Roe v. Wade was decided in 1973.
The matter of Acme Corp v. Beta Industries was settled out of court.
Brown v. Board of Education changed everything.

cases.spec: Extract the parties from the case name into a JSON object with exactly the keys "plaintiff" and "defendant". Output only the JSON.

cases.name: NER: stock tickers

queries.input: AAPL and MSFT closed higher while TSLA dipped.
Should I buy NVDA before earnings?
Rotated out of GOOG into AMZN last week.
thinking of yolo-ing my savings into GME again 💎🙌

cases.spec: Extract all stock ticker symbols from the text, comma-separated.

cases.name: NER: monetary amounts

queries.input: Revenue rose from $1.2 billion to $1.5 billion.
The fine was €50,000 plus another $10,000 in fees.
He still owes me twenty bucks.

cases.spec: Extract all monetary amounts from the text, comma-separated, exactly as written.

cases.name: NER: programming languages and frameworks

queries.input: We migrated the backend from Django to FastAPI.
She knows Python, Rust, and a bit of TypeScript.
the frontend is react but we're eyeing svelte
Legacy code in COBOL still runs the core banking system.

cases.spec: Extract all programming languages and frameworks mentioned, comma-separated.

cases.name: NER: all dates to ISO

queries.input: The project started on 2026-01-15 and shipped on 2026-06-30.
Book flights for March 3, 2027 and return on March 10, 2027.

cases.spec: Extract all dates mentioned in the text, comma-separated, each in YYYY-MM-DD format.

cases.name: NER: nested organization names

queries.input: She studies at the University of California, Berkeley.
He works for the Bank of England.
The Museum of Modern Art is closed on Mondays.
They toured the University of Tokyo campus.

cases.spec: Extract the full organization name from the text, including any place name that is part of it. Output only the organization name.

cases.name: NER: job titles

queries.input: Chief Financial Officer Maria Ruiz resigned yesterday.
They hired a senior data engineer last week.
As VP of Sales, Tom oversees three regions.
she's a paediatric nurse at the children's hospital

cases.spec: Extract the person's job title from the text. Output only the title, as written.

cases.name: NER: product names

queries.input: The iPhone 15 outsold the Galaxy S24 in the third quarter.
I traded my PlayStation 5 for a Nintendo Switch.
just copped the new air jordans 🔥
The Model Y remains Tesla's best seller.

cases.spec: Extract all product names from the text, comma-separated, as written.

cases.name: NER: named events

queries.input: She won gold at the 2024 Paris Olympics.
Tickets for Coachella sold out in minutes.
The keynote at WWDC ran for two hours.
We first met at Oktoberfest years ago.

cases.spec: Extract the named event from the text. Output only the event name.

cases.name: NER: animal species

queries.input: The reserve protects Bengal tigers and Asian elephants.
saw a huge huntsman spider in the shed 😱
Blue whales and humpback whales migrate along this coast.
A red fox darted across the road.

cases.spec: Extract all animal species mentioned in the text, comma-separated.

cases.name: NER: person names in noisy social text

queries.input: omg just bumped into rihanna at the mall no way
tbh i think zendaya and tom holland are the cutest couple
keanu reeves is such a nice guy fr fr
u reckon messi is better than ronaldo??

cases.spec: Extract all person names from the informal text, comma-separated, using the names as written in the text but with proper capitalization.

cases.name: Price extraction as decimal

queries.input: They quoted us $450.00 for the repair.
they want like 350 bucks for it, crazy right?

cases.spec: Extract the price from the text as a decimal number of dollars, without a currency symbol.

cases.name: Email address extraction

queries.input: Contact us at support@acme.com for help.
Send your resume to jobs@example.org before Friday.
My work address is jane.doe+news@mail.co.uk, use that one.
You can reach the team via dev-team@startup.io anytime.
shoot me an email at bob_99@hotmail.com whenever

cases.spec: Extract the email address from the text. Output only the email address.

cases.name: Date extraction to ISO format

queries.input: We launched on 2025-11-30 to great reviews.
Their anniversary is on the 4th of July, 2026.
we're getting married on the 14th of Feb 2027!!

cases.spec: Extract the date mentioned in the text and output it in ISO format YYYY-MM-DD.

cases.name: Phone number extraction (digits only)
cases.spec: Extract the phone number from the text as digits only, with no spaces or punctuation.

cases.name: Person name extraction

queries.input: The report was written by Dr. Jane Goodall last year.
CEO Tim Cook announced the results on stage.
Please forward this to Ms. Maria Garcia in accounting.
The award went to Professor Alan Turing.
so i met serena williams at the airport lol
The novel was penned by Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie.

cases.spec: Extract the person's full name from the text, without titles or honorifics.

cases.name: URL extraction

queries.input: the link is https://a.co/d/xyz123 if u want it

cases.spec: Extract the URL from the text. Output only the URL.

cases.name: Hashtag extraction
cases.spec: Extract all hashtags from the text, comma-separated.

cases.name: JSON key lookup
cases.spec: Given a JSON object, output the value of its top-level "status" key.

cases.name: CSV second column extraction
cases.spec: Output the value in the second column of the comma-separated row.

cases.name: JSON repair (quotes and trailing commas)
cases.spec: Fix malformed JSON: add missing double quotes around keys and strings, remove trailing commas. Output only valid JSON.

cases.name: JSON repair (Python literals)
cases.spec: Convert Python-style dict literals to valid JSON: True/False/None become true/false/null and single quotes become double quotes. Output only valid JSON.

cases.name: JSON repair (missing closing brackets)
cases.spec: Add the missing closing brackets and braces to make the input valid JSON. Output only the repaired JSON.

cases.name: CSV whitespace repair
cases.spec: Clean the comma-separated line by removing spaces around commas and at the ends. Output only the cleaned line.

cases.name: Spelling correction
cases.spec: Correct the spelling mistakes in the sentence. Output only the corrected sentence.

cases.name: CSV row to JSON object
cases.spec: Convert a CSV row with columns name,age,city into a JSON object with keys "name" (string), "age" (integer), and "city" (string). Output only the JSON.

cases.name: Key=value pairs to JSON
cases.spec: Convert semicolon-separated key=value pairs into a JSON object with string values. Example: "a=1; b=two" -> {"a": "1", "b": "two"}. Output only the JSON.

cases.name: Phrase to snake_case
cases.spec: Convert the input phrase to snake_case (lowercase words joined by underscores). Output only the result.

cases.name: US date to ISO date
cases.spec: Convert the date from MM/DD/YYYY format to YYYY-MM-DD format. Output only the converted date.

cases.name: Last-comma-first to first-last
cases.spec: Convert a name in "Last, First" format to "First Last". Output only the converted name.

cases.name: Time to 24-hour format
cases.spec: Convert the time to 24-hour HH:MM format with a leading zero when needed. Output only the time.

cases.name: Strip HTML tags
cases.spec: Remove all HTML tags from the input. Output only the plain text.

cases.name: Currency to ISO code
cases.spec: Output the ISO 4217 currency code for the currency mentioned in the text.

cases.name: Company name canonicalization
cases.spec: Map the messy company name to its canonical name, one of: Apple, Google, Microsoft, Amazon. Output only the canonical name.

cases.name: Country name normalization
cases.spec: Normalize the country reference to its canonical name, one of: United States, United Kingdom, Netherlands, Germany. Output only the canonical name.

cases.name: Fuzzy yes/no normalization
cases.spec: Interpret the reply as agreement or refusal. Output only yes or no.

cases.name: Nearest basic color
cases.spec: Map the color description to the nearest basic color, one of: red, blue, green, yellow, black, white. Output only the basic color.

cases.name: Informal day reference to weekday

queries.input: the start of the working week
the day of rest, sunday roast day

cases.spec: Map the informal reference to the weekday it describes. Output only the weekday name.

cases.name: Product category matching
cases.spec: Classify the product into one of: electronics, clothing, food, furniture. Output only the category.

cases.name: Support ticket triage

queries.input: I was charged twice this month.
The app crashes when I upload a photo.
I forgot my password and can't log in.
My invoice shows the wrong amount.
hi um my card got charged but i never got a confirmation email??
site keeps throwing a 500 error when i hit checkout

cases.spec: Route the support ticket to exactly one team: billing, technical, account, or general. Output only the team name.

cases.name: Incident severity routing (rule spec)

queries.input: Production database is down for all users.
Reports page loads slowly for some users.
We lost customer data during last night's migration.
Button color looks slightly off in dark mode.
Checkout is completely broken, nobody can buy anything.
Search results take 15 seconds to load since yesterday.

cases.spec: Classify the incident severity using these rules: outages or data loss are critical; degraded performance is high; cosmetic issues are low. Output exactly one of: critical, high, low.

cases.name: Employee question routing

queries.input: How many vacation days do I have left?
When will expense reimbursements be paid out?
The air conditioning is too cold on level 3.
I need access to the shared drive.
who do i talk to about parental leave?
the wifi on floor 2 keeps dropping

cases.spec: Route the employee question to the right department: HR, IT, Finance, or Facilities. Output only the department name.

cases.name: Escalation decision (two-condition rule)
cases.spec: Decide whether to escalate a support case. Escalate only if the customer is angry AND the case has been open for more than 7 days; otherwise respond normally. Output exactly one word: escalate or respond.

cases.name: Smart home command routing

queries.input: dim the living room a bit
it's freezing in here, bump it up two degrees
did i lock the front door?
turn everything off in the bedroom... actually just the lamp

cases.spec: Route the voice command to the right device: lights, thermostat, speaker, or lock. Output only the device name.

cases.name: English pluralization (with irregulars)
cases.spec: Output the plural form of the given English noun.

cases.name: Verb to past tense
cases.spec: Output the simple past tense of the given English verb.

cases.name: Title case conversion
cases.spec: Capitalize the first letter of every word in the input. Output only the converted text.

cases.name: Order sentence rewrite
cases.spec: Rewrite input of the form "<name> ordered <item>" as "<item> for <name>". Example: "Sam ordered tea" -> "tea for Sam". Output only the rewritten text.

cases.name: Abbreviation expansion
cases.spec: Expand the abbreviation into its full standard English phrase. Output only the phrase.

cases.name: PII redaction
cases.spec: Replace every email address and phone number in the text with the literal text [REDACTED], keeping the rest of the sentence unchanged. Output only the result.

cases.name: Contact details to JSON

queries.input: Reach John Smith at john@example.com for details.
Ana Lopez (ana.lopez@mail.net) will coordinate the event.
Email Marcus Chen on marcus@corp.io with any questions.
Our contact is Priya Patel, priya.patel@example.org.
hey it's Sam Lee btw, you can email me at sam.lee@gmail.com

cases.spec: Extract contact details from the text into a JSON object with exactly the keys "name" and "email" (both strings). Output only the JSON.

cases.name: Order quantities to JSON
cases.spec: Parse the order into a JSON object mapping each item name to its integer quantity. Example: "2 pens and 1 ruler" -> {"pens": 2, "ruler": 1}. Output only the JSON.

cases.name: Property search filters to JSON

queries.input: looking for a 2br in Coogee, can spend up to 1.2m

cases.spec: Extract property search filters into a JSON object with exactly the keys "suburb" (string), "beds" (integer), and "max_price" (integer dollars). Output only the JSON.

cases.name: Event details to JSON
cases.spec: Extract event details into a JSON object with exactly the keys "title" (string), "date" (YYYY-MM-DD), and "time" (HH:MM, 24-hour). Output only the JSON.

cases.name: One sentence on a topic

queries.output: Databases are structured collections of data used to store, retrieve, and manage information.
Coffee is a drink made by heating water with coffee beans.
Bicycles are two-wheeled vehicles used for transportation on roads.
Rain is a natural phenomenon that brings water to the ground.
Guitars are musical instruments that play a role in many genres of music.

cases.spec: Write one short sentence about the given topic. The sentence must contain the topic word exactly as given.

cases.name: Question generation about a topic
cases.spec: Write one question about the given topic. The question must contain the topic word exactly as given and end with a question mark.

cases.name: Exactly three words
cases.spec: Describe the given animal in exactly three words.

cases.name: List three habitat animals
cases.spec: List exactly three animals that live in the given habitat, separated by commas. Output only the list.

cases.name: Single-word answers

queries.input: Describe the ocean in one word.
One word for a baby dog?
Sum up Mondays in one word.
one word: how does pizza taste?

cases.spec: Answer the prompt with exactly one word.

cases.name: Capital city lookup
cases.spec: Output the capital city of the given country.

cases.name: Chemical element symbol
cases.spec: Output the chemical symbol for the given element.

cases.name: Antonym lookup
cases.spec: Output the antonym (opposite) of the given word.

cases.name: Factual yes/no questions

queries.input: Was the Eiffel Tower built before 1800?
can u breathe underwater without equipment?

cases.spec: Answer the factual question with only yes or no.

cases.name: Planet identification from clue

queries.input: the largest planet in the solar system
the planet famous for its rings

cases.spec: Output the name of the planet described by the clue.

cases.name: Add two numbers
cases.spec: Add the two numbers and output only the sum.

cases.name: Subtract two numbers
cases.spec: Subtract the second number from the first and output only the result.

cases.name: Multiply small numbers
cases.spec: Multiply the two numbers and output only the product.

cases.name: Percentage calculation
cases.spec: Calculate the percentage and output only the resulting number.

cases.name: Number to English words
cases.spec: Convert the number to its English word form, e.g. 3 -> three. Output only the words.

cases.name: Count list items

queries.input: milk, eggs, bread, butter, jam, tea

cases.spec: Count how many items are in the comma-separated list. Output only the number.

cases.name: Largest number in list
cases.spec: Output the largest number in the comma-separated list.

cases.name: Roman numeral conversion
cases.spec: Convert the Roman numeral to a regular number. Output only the number.

cases.name: Reverse a string
cases.spec: Reverse the characters of the input string. Output only the reversed string.

cases.name: Sort letters alphabetically

queries.output: a b a n a n a

cases.spec: Sort the letters of the input word into alphabetical order. Output only the sorted letters.

cases.name: Count letter occurrences
cases.spec: Count how many times the letter "a" appears in the input word. Output only the number.

cases.name: Acronym from phrase
cases.spec: Output the acronym formed by the first letter of each word in the phrase.

cases.name: Count words in a sentence

queries.input: to be or not to be

cases.spec: Count the words in the sentence. Output only the number.

cases.name: Two-hop transitive reasoning

queries.input: Alice is taller than Bob. Bob is taller than Carol. Who is the tallest?
Rex is older than Spot. Spot is older than Fido. Who is the youngest?
The red box is heavier than the blue box. The blue box is heavier than the green box. Which box is the lightest?
Tom finished before Jerry. Jerry finished before Sam. Who finished last?
Maya runs faster than Liam. Liam runs faster than Noah. Who is the slowest?
Box A costs more than Box B. Box C costs less than Box B. Which box is the cheapest?

cases.spec: Answer the question using the facts given. Output only the name.

cases.name: Day-of-week offset
cases.spec: Work out which day of the week it will be. Output only the day name.

cases.name: Chained shipping-cost rules
cases.spec: Compute the shipping cost in dollars using these rules: orders over $100 ship free (cost 0); otherwise domestic orders cost $10 and international orders cost $25. Output only the cost as a number.

cases.name: Date arithmetic (one week later)
cases.spec: Output the date exactly one week after the given date, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

cases.name: Meeting end time
cases.spec: The meeting starts at the given time and runs for 45 minutes. Output the end time in HH:MM 24-hour format.

cases.name: Second largest number
cases.spec: Output the second largest number in the comma-separated list.

cases.name: Oldest person from facts

queries.input: Amy is 34, Ben is 41, Cara is 29.
Leo is 8, his mum is 38, and his grandpa is 71.
the twins are 12 and their sister Zoe is 15

cases.spec: Given people and their ages, output the name of the oldest person.

cases.name: Boolean expression evaluation
cases.spec: Evaluate the boolean expression. Output only true or false.
